outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: ASML jumped roughly 7% after hiking its sales forecast for the second time this year on strong AI chip demand, echoing TSMC's 36% quarterly sales jump reported earlier this week. Memory names also firmed up as SK Hynix-linked leveraged ETFs launched, lifting Micron alongside SanDisk and Western Digital.

CrowdStrike and Zscaler both remain up roughly 9-10% following the joint US/international advisory on Russian state-sponsored threats to critical infrastructure, with an added tailwind from enterprise clients reportedly reprioritizing security spending over other software.

weakest.reason: Salesforce fell after a KeyBanc downgrade citing weak customer feedback on its Agentforce AI product, and Adobe dropped over 4% today. ServiceNow has only partly recovered from yesterday's IBM-driven pullback, and the broader group is still digesting IBM's warning that clients are pausing software deals.
ORCL extended its slide to a fresh multi-month low, a separate, company-specific overhang tied to concerns about its debt load and execution risk on its AI data-center buildout.

outlook.nextStep: ASML's second sales-forecast hike this year, paired with TSMC's growth numbers, is worth a closer look as evidence that AI capex demand remains durable even as individual names stay volatile; the Iran-driven oil spike and Tesla-SpaceX merger speculation are better watched than acted on given how headline- and geopolitics-driven both are right now.

signals.title: Renewed Iran strikes push oil higher even as cool CPI eases rate fears
signals.body: Trump vowed 'very hard' strikes on Iran, pushing WTI crude up over 1.5% to around $80.54, even as June CPI (3.5% YoY, below the 3.8% expected) continues to cut July rate-hike odds — CME FedWatch now shows roughly 17% odds of a July hike, down from over 40% a few days ago. The combination of easing core inflation and rising energy-driven headline risk is a bit of a tug-of-war for markets.
signals.watch: Whether the Hormuz Strait rhetoric translates into actual supply disruption or stays rhetorical.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — the oil/geopolitics angle could offset some of the rate-cut optimism if it escalates.

signals.title: ASML hikes its sales forecast for the second time this year
signals.body: ASML jumped roughly 7% after raising its sales guidance again on strong AI chip demand, reported the same day as TSMC's 36% quarterly sales jump and 67.9% YoY June revenue growth. Memory names also got a lift as SK Hynix-linked leveraged ETFs launched, boosting Micron, SanDisk, and Western Digital.
signals.watch: Whether other equipment and foundry names confirm the same demand signal in upcoming earnings (Cadence reports July 27).
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — two separate data points (ASML guidance, TSMC revenue) both point the same direction on AI capex durability.

signals.title: Oracle extends its slide to a fresh multi-month low
signals.body: ORCL continued lower today, extending a stretch of underperformance tied to concerns about the company's debt load and execution risk on its AI data-center buildout — a company-specific overhang distinct from the broader software pullback hitting the rest of the sector.
signals.watch: Any updated guidance from Oracle on data-center capex timelines or debt management in its next earnings update.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — worth waiting for a clearer signal on the debt/execution concerns before drawing conclusions.

signals.title: Salesforce downgraded on weak Agentforce AI feedback; software stays soft
signals.body: KeyBanc downgraded Salesforce citing weak customer feedback on its Agentforce AI product, sending CRM down over 2%. Adobe fell more than 4% the same day. Both moves extend the broader enterprise-software weakness that followed IBM's Q2 warning about clients pausing software deals to prioritize security and hardware spending.
signals.watch: Whether other AI-agent products from SaaS vendors draw similar customer-feedback concerns in upcoming earnings calls.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — a specific, named product concern (not just macro caution) is a different kind of signal than the broader IBM-driven pullback.

signals.title: JPMorgan floats a Tesla-SpaceX merger idea ahead of Q2 earnings
signals.body: JPMorgan analysts called a potential Tesla-SpaceX merger 'strategically coherent' in a note this week, even as TSLA itself fell over 3% today ahead of its July 22 Q2 earnings report. Teradyne also pulled back sharply after its roughly 140% YTD run on AI-chip testing and Universal Robots demand.
signals.watch: Whether any concrete merger discussion materializes, or whether this stays speculative analyst commentary.
signals.focus: Speculative — this is analyst speculation, not confirmed corporate action, and shouldn't be treated as a near-term catalyst.

signals.title: Bitcoin and Ethereum extend gains on cooler inflation data
signals.body: Bitcoin traded near $64,630 (+3.3%) and Ethereum near $1,792 (+2.4%), both continuing to benefit from the cooler June CPI print and reduced near-term Fed rate-hike odds.
signals.watch: Whether crypto holds these gains if the Iran-driven oil/inflation risk re-accelerates in the coming days.
signals.focus: Worth checking in on — the move looks tied to the same rate-cut optimism lifting equities, not a crypto-specific catalyst.

signals.title: Cybersecurity rally holds after Russian state-threat advisory
signals.body: CrowdStrike and Zscaler remain up roughly 9-10% following the joint US/international advisory on Russian state-sponsored threats to critical infrastructure, with both now trading well clear of their tracked stops.
signals.watch: Whether the move fades as the news cycle passes, or whether it holds as a genuine budget-reallocation signal echoing IBM's commentary on client spending shifts.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look, but be mindful the move is now a few sessions old — most of the initial reaction may already be priced in.
